title: Vi Vision Lanes Archetype (render-type)
description:
The LANES archetype — an instance of archetype.schema.json (render_kind='lanes'): renders TIME × ENTITY as horizontal lanes with event marks (NOT a log). A FACE-1 surface archetype.

WHAT IT RENDERS: a lane per entity (e.g. a session) + event marks along a time axis + a now-marker. Grounded in REAL usage — the timeline (a lane per session · compaction/event marks · drift fork-points · ordinal-warmth by recency/kind), the fleet-as-lanes. Bound to the now-LIVE /api/timeline (fork's FINAL shape: 200 + timeline:null + a reason for an honest empty-state, NOT a 400; 400 only if ?session is missing) — so the lanes archetype renders an HONEST empty-state, never an error, when there's no timeline yet. DNA renders (the lane organism); the resolver places (wide = full lanes; narrow = active lanes + a collapse).

Mostly READ; an optional take opens a mark/lane (→ the session-card).

LAWS (inherited from archetype.schema): operator-legibility (lanes labelled by the entity's human name; marks read their kind by meaning/warmth) · DNA-token-built (ordinal warmth) · resolver-placed · navigable-not-text · native dual-device · honest empty-state (no-silent-failure / no-error-on-empty).

lanes.description: The LANE source — an entity per lane (e.g. a session; each with its human name).

marks.description: The EVENT-MARK source — events along time per lane (each with its kind + recency → ordinal warmth). Honest empty-state when none (the /api/timeline null+reason shape).

axis.description: Optional: the TIME-AXIS source (the time scale).
